title: paspalum sanguinale var. cruciatum
intro: Paspalum sanguinale var. cruciatum, commonly known as the purpletop, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Paspalum sanguinale var. cruciatum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared by drying and infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea, or by making a decoction of the roots and leaves.
